AUTH.OIDC_DISCOVERY_FAILED: OIDC is enabled, but the provider authorization endpoint could not be discovered. Check the issuer URL in settings.
AUTH.OIDC_ENDPOINT_UNAVAILABLE: OIDC is enabled but the authorization endpoint is unavailable. Check the issuer URL and provider discovery document.
AUTH.CB_INVALID_STATE: Invalid OIDC state — possible CSRF attempt

ABOUT.TITLE: About
ABOUT.BUILT_WITH: Built with Angular 21 (Zoneless + Signals) + FastAPI. Manages CNCF Distribution (Docker Registry).
ABOUT.FEATURES: Features: Browse images, manage tags, staging pipeline with optional ClamAV scanning, optional Trivy CVE scanning, OIDC support.

TOKENS.INFO_PRE: Tokens are stored securely as bcrypt hashes. The raw value is only shown once at creation time. You can use either the full token or a dedicated 16-character short token for

EXTREG.TITLE: External Registries
EXTREG.DESC: Saved registries used for push and sync operations.
EXTREG.HTTP_WARN: Plain HTTP — credentials will be transmitted unencrypted. Use only on isolated/trusted networks.

VULN.SCANNER_DESC: Scans every pulled image for known vulnerabilities (CVEs) before allowing a push to the registry. When an override is active, these settings are saved server-side and applied to all users.
VULN.ENABLE_SCAN_DESC: Run CVE scan before allowing push
VULN.IGNORE_UNFIXED_DESC: Do not block on CVEs with no available fix

OIDC.TITLE: OIDC Configuration
OIDC.INTRO: Users will be able to log in via your OIDC provider on the login page. The env-based admin login remains available as a fallback — unless OIDC-only mode is enabled below, which disables every local login.
OIDC.ISSUER_HELP_PRE: Base URL of your OIDC provider (Keycloak, Authentik, Dex, etc.). Portalcrane will append
OIDC.SECRET_HELP: Leave empty to keep the current stored value.
OIDC.REDIRECT_HELP: Must match exactly the redirect URI registered in your OIDC provider.
OIDC.ACCESS_CONTROL_INTRO: Map your provider's groups to Portalcrane roles, or manage roles manually from the users panel.
OIDC.GROUP_MAPPING_OFF_HELP: Group mapping is off: OIDC users keep the role assigned to them in the users panel, and it is preserved across logins. Turn this on to derive roles from your provider's groups instead.
OIDC.ADMIN_GROUP_HELP: Group defined in your OIDC provider: when an SSO user carries this value in the Admin group claim, they are granted the admin role. Portalcrane has only two roles — admin and user — so this maps a provider group to the admin role; it does not create groups in Portalcrane.
OIDC.REGULAR_USERS_HELP: Optional. When an SSO user carries the value below in the given claim, they are granted the user role. Enable Restrict access to mapped groups below to turn OIDC access into an allowlist.
OIDC.USER_GROUP_HELP: Group defined in your OIDC provider: when an SSO user carries this value in the User group claim, they are granted the user role. This does not grant admin — use the Admins fields above for that.
OIDC.RESTRICT_GROUPS_HELP: When enabled, OIDC access becomes an allowlist: only admins and users matching the mappings above may log in. Any SSO user belonging to neither the admin nor the user group is denied and their account is not created.
OIDC.OIDC_ONLY_HELP: Disables every local password login, including the built-in env-admin. Requires either the admin group mapping above or an OIDC account already promoted to admin.
OIDC.OIDC_ONLY_WARN: OIDC-only mode needs a reachable admin: set the admin group mapping (Admin group claim and Admin group), or promote an existing OIDC account to admin first. Saving will be rejected otherwise to prevent a lockout.
OIDC.TEST_TITLE: Contact the OIDC provider and check that the required information can be retrieved
OIDC.TEST_SUCCESS: Connection to the OIDC provider succeeded.
OIDC.TEST_FAILED: Connection test failed — see the details below.
OIDC.INFO_MID: and take effect immediately — no restart required. Environment variables
OIDC.INFO_POST: serve as default values and are overridden by the values saved here.

ACCOUNTS.PWD_MIN: Password must be at least 8 characters
ACCOUNTS.TITLE: Local Accounts
ACCOUNTS.DESC: Create users and grant administrative rights.
ACCOUNTS.PERMS_INFO: Pull and push permissions are granted in the Folders tab after creating the user.
ACCOUNTS.ENV_BADGE_TITLE: Built-in admin account. The password is auto-generated on first launch and shown once in the server logs.
ACCOUNTS.OIDC_BADGE_TITLE: Account provisioned automatically on first SSO login. Password is managed by the identity provider.
ACCOUNTS.LOCAL_BADGE_TITLE: Local account with password stored in Portalcrane.
ACCOUNTS.OIDC_INFO: This account is authenticated via an external identity provider. The password is managed by the provider and cannot be changed here. You can only modify the admin role.
ACCOUNTS.ENV_INFO: Built-in admin account. Only the password can be changed here — the account is always an administrator and cannot be deleted. The new password takes effect immediately.
ACCOUNTS.LEGEND: The built-in admin account (badge ENV) has a password that is auto-generated on first launch and shown once in the server logs — you can change it here, but the account is always an administrator and cannot be deleted. OIDC accounts are provisioned automatically on first SSO login — deleting one revokes future access.

NETWORK.PROXY_DESC: Override the container OS proxy environment variables at runtime. Affects Docker Hub API calls, OIDC discovery, and skopeo operations.
NETWORK.PROXY_ENV_POST: Enable the override above to customize values.
NETWORK.PROXY_AUTH_HINT: (credentials are embedded in the proxy URL)
NETWORK.SYSLOG_DESC: Forward audit logs and/or Uvicorn access logs to a remote syslog server. Supports UDP (RFC 3164/5424), TCP plain, and TCP+TLS (RFC 5425). Authentication is available for TCP-based connections (RFC 6587).
NETWORK.EMAIL_DESC: Deliver the audit log by email through an SMTP server. Configure the mail server below, then send the log on demand or test connectivity.
NETWORK.SUBJECT: Message subject
NETWORK.SMTP_AUTH_HINT: (leave empty if the server requires no credentials)

FOLDERS.TITLE: Registry Folders
FOLDERS.DESC: Creating directories allows you to organize images and assign permissions to users.
FOLDERS.ROOT_INFO: This folder controls access to images with no namespace prefix (e.g. nginx, ubuntu) and to images whose prefix does not match any configured folder. It is created automatically and cannot be deleted.

GROUPS.TITLE: Groups
GROUPS.DESC: Create groups and assign users, then grant folder pull/push permissions to groups.
GROUPS.LEGEND: Folder permissions are granted to groups. A user's access is the union of the permissions of the groups they belong to.
